name: Brunswick Stew
recipeIngredient: 48 ounces canned whole tomatoes (undrained and chopped)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the roast, fat side up, on a rack in a roasting pan. Insert a meat thermometer and bake at 325°F for about 30 to 35 minutes per pound or until the thermometer reads 160°F. Cool. Trim and discard the fat; cut the pork into 2-inch pieces.
2. Place the hen in a Dutch oven and cover with water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 2 hours or until tender. Remove the hen from the broth and cool (reserve the broth). Bone the hen and cut the meat into 2-inch pieces.
3. Coarsely grind the pork and chicken in a food processor or with a meat grinder.
4. Combine the ground meat, tomatoes, tomato sauce, onions, green peppers, vinegar, and sugar in a large Dutch oven.
5. In a separate bowl, combine flour and water until smooth; stir into the meat mixture. Stir in salt, pepper, turmeric, and hot sauce.
6. Cook over medium heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add water if needed to reach the desired consistency. Stir in corn and cook an additional 10 minutes.
Yield: 22 cups
Note: Brunswick Stew freezes well. To serve, thaw and cook until thoroughly heated.
